strategy_notes: CONSOLIDATION PHASE — NO NEW ADDS. Key principles: (1) All MACD signals remain bearish across entire universe — rally is technically unconfirmed. Do NOT add new positions or pyramid further until at least 2-3 names show MACD bullish crossovers. (2) Current allocation ~77% invested, 23% cash — this is at the upper limit for an unconfirmed rally. (3) Trump address tonight = event risk — no action before clarity. (4) Maintain 6% hard stops on all positions. If SPY drops >2% on any day, trim ALL positions by 50% immediately. (5) AMZN is the weakest position — first trim candidate if portfolio needs derisking. (6) Profit-taking rule: take 50% off any position reaching +7% from entry. (7) The Day 2 pause is normal after sharp rallies, but Day 3-4 must show follow-through or this is a dead cat bounce. (8) Research showed fixed percentage targets (5-7%) outperform trailing stops in MACD-unconfirmed rallies — implement this.

recent_trades.reason: Adding to NVDA position using pyramiding approach. NVDA +5.59% today, leading the semiconductor rally. AI/datacenter secular tailwind is intact. If the Iran de-escalation narrative holds, growth/cyclical tech will continue leading. This is a confirmation add — the initial entry was yesterday and it's already confirming with strong momentum. Scaling up per pyramiding research: add to winners showing confirmation.

Adding to META position. +6.67% today — strongest performer in the top 50. Mega-cap tech is leading this rotation with strong ad revenue fundamentals and AI investments. Pyramiding into a winner that's confirming. If this rally has legs, META will be among the biggest beneficiaries of a sentiment shift.

GOOGL +5.14% today — strong participation in the tech rally. Diversifying cyclical exposure across mega-cap tech rather than concentrating in just two names. GOOGL benefits from same AI/advertising tailwinds as META but adds search/cloud diversification. Initial position size per pyramiding strategy — start moderate, add on confirmation next cycle.

ABBV -0.94% today on a +1.06% SPY day — significant relative weakness. Defensive healthcare is clearly rotating out as the market shifts to cyclical/growth leadership. Still +2.41% from entry, so locking in a small win. This frees ~$12.5K for better-positioned names. Following the proven pattern: sell defensives showing negative relative strength on rally days (same signal that correctly flagged COST and XOM exits).

AMZN +2.11% today — participating strongly in the cyclical rally. Diversifies tech exposure beyond pure advertising (META/GOOGL) into e-commerce/cloud (AWS). AMZN benefits from consumer spending recovery if geopolitical resolution boosts confidence. Starting at 18% per pyramiding approach — moderate initial size, will add on confirmation next cycle. This fills the 5th position slot freed by ABBV sale.

last_decision.market_assessment: Market is flat/consolidating after the strong April 1st rally. SPY unchanged, QQQ slightly negative. ALL MACD signals remain bearish across every stock in the universe — this is a critical warning that the rally is unconfirmed technically. Trump's televised address tonight creates significant event risk. The initial bounce from the geopolitical de-escalation may be stalling, and headlines about inflation/rate worries suggest the macro backdrop remains uncertain. This is a classic Day 2 pause after a sharp relief rally — the next 1-2 sessions will determine if this becomes a trend reversal or a dead cat bounce.
last_decision.self_reflection: My cyclical tech rotation on April 1st was well-timed — I caught the big rally day and sold ABBV at the right moment (+2.36%). However, I'm now sitting with 4 concentrated mega-cap tech positions that are ALL showing slight losses or barely positive P&L since entry, and EVERY single MACD signal is bearish. My research on bear market rally identification warned that volume confirmation and breadth expansion are needed — I haven't seen that yet. The pyramiding approach added to NVDA and META at higher levels, which increases risk if this is just a bounce. My 75% invested / 25% cash split is at the upper end of my comfort zone given the unconfirmed technical setup. Key lesson: the initial rally was strong enough to justify entry, but Day 2 consolidation with universally bearish MACD means I should NOT add more here — I should set clear exit triggers and be prepared to trim if momentum fades.

decisions.reason: NVDA +0.37% today, holding gains from rally day. Still below entry on the latest add ($175.13 avg), but showing slight positive momentum. MACD bearish is a concern but AI/datacenter secular thesis intact. Holding with tight 6% stop from current price ($165.80). Will reassess next cycle — need to see MACD start converging or continued price strength.

META -0.57% today, slight giveback after the massive +6.67% rally day. Still near entry ($578.23 avg vs $575.80 current = -0.42%). Analyst consensus target of $837 suggests significant upside if market stabilizes. Holding but this is the first position I'd trim if broad market weakens — it's the largest position at ~$20.4K. 6% stop at ~$542.
GOOGL is actually the best performer among my positions at +1.92% from entry, showing relative strength. Analyst target $351 is ~18% upside. Holding as the strongest position. 6% stop at ~$280.

AMZN -0.74% from entry, the weakest of my 4 positions. However, it's only Day 2 and the drawdown is minimal. AWS cloud growth and e-commerce provide diversification within tech. If AMZN doesn't recover to breakeven within 2 more sessions, it becomes the first trim candidate. 6% stop at ~$199.

last_decision.next_research: Event-driven trading around presidential addresses and major policy announcements — specifically how markets react in the 24-48 hours after presidential primetime addresses, whether pre-event positioning or post-event reaction trading is more profitable, and how to manage overnight gap risk in concentrated tech portfolios
